sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.79 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: Suppose that$a$and$b$are two elements of a finite group$G$such that the function %$$(g)=1^G(g) - 1_{ < a >}^G(g) - 1_{ < b >}^G(g) - 1_{ < ab >}^G(g) + 2$%$is a character of$G$. Is it true that$G= < a,b >$? The converse statement is true. { S.\,P.\,Strunkov} No, not always: a counterexample is given by$G=A_4$,$a=b=(123)$. (S.\,V.\,Skresanov, Algebra Logic, { 58}, no.\,3 (2019), 249--253).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
